ogTitle: Hickeys Creek vs Higher Macdonald: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hickeys Creek and Higher Macdonald.
heading: Hickeys Creek vs Higher Macdonald — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hickeys Creek, New South Wales and Higher Macdonald, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hickeys Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Higher Macdonald (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hickeys Creek or Higher Macdonald safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hickeys Creek has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Higher Macdonald scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hickeys Creek scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Higher Macdonald scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hickeys Creek or Higher Macdonald?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hickeys Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
